LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER IS GRANTED. THE LIBERAL POLICY TO ALLOW AMENDMENT AND HEAR CASES ON THE MERITS OUTWEIGHS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMED PREJUDICE. DEFENDANTS SHALL FILE AND SERVE THE AMENDED ANSWER WITHIN 5 DAYS OF THIS HEARING. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/ACM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT [Civ. Proc. Code § 437c]
Off Calendar Nov-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Hogue, Esq. 415-655-1303 of GreenbergTraurig for Plaintiff AAMCO TRANSMISSIONS, INC.
Mik Ewing, Esq. 707-263-6400 of Ewing & Associates for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues 3 key points. Defendant argues this is not the time to decide the facts. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF AAMCO TRANSMISSIONS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT [Civ. Proc. Code 437c].

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Aamco's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Aamco's claim is barred by the doctrines of unclean hands/economic duress. Aamco (as assignee) is subject to any defense that defendants could have raised against the assignor. (See YDM Management Co. Inc. v. Sharp Community Medical Group, Inc. (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 613, 621 fn. 3.) In addition, Aamco is subject to the unclean hands defense based on the alleged wrongdoing of its agent, Mr. O'Donnell. (See Restatement 2nd of Contracts 336(4).) Here, Mr. O'Donnell's statement that defendants' proposed franchise "more than likely" would be subject to the single point scheme was negligent and misleading. Defendants' evidence shows that Mr. O'Donnell had the precise A.C. Nielsen information easily available but failed to disclose it to defendants. (Szupello Dec., par. 17; O'Donnell Depo., 23:3-24:14; Phillips Depo., 24:24-27:12; 29:2-30:23.) Mr. O'Donnell should have provided the precise information or said nothing misleading at all. The unclean hands defense does not require a showing of fraud. "Any conduct that violates conscience, or good faith, or other equitable standards of conduct is sufficient cause to invoke the doctrine." (Kendall-Jackson Winery, Ltd. v. Sup. Crt. (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 970, 979.) The unclean hands defense is available to an action at law. (Id. at 978.) The court rejects plaintiff's argument that Mr. O'Donnell's statement was mere opinion. First, as noted, the unclean hands defense is a broad equitable doctrine that does not require "fraud." Second, Mr. O'Donnell's statement is analogous to statements that have supported fraud/negligent misrepresentation claims. (See Hobart v. Hobart Estate Co. (1945) 26 Cal.2d 412, 433-434 ("This court has held that a false statement of opinion fraudulently made may form the basis of an action where the party making it possesses superior knowledge or special information regarding the subject matter of the representation."); Edward Barron Estate Co. v. Woodruff Co. (1912) 163 Cal. 561, 574 (statement by architect that a building will not cost more than a certain amount may be regarded as an affirmation of fact.).) Lastly, on a related note, the economic duress defense is also viable in the instant circumstances. (See Rich & Whillock, Inc. v. Aston Development, Inc. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 1154, 1158.)

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' motion for leave to file an amended answer is GRANTED. The liberal policy to allow amendment and hear cases on the merits outweighs plaintiff's claimed prejudice. Defendants shall file and serve the amended answer within 5 days of this hearing. Order signed. (see order for details)
